title: viburnum mullaha var. involucratum
intro: Viburnum mullaha var. involucratum, also known as Himalayan cranberry, is a species of flowering plant in the honeysuckle family, native to the Himalayas. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Viburnum mullaha var. involucratum include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and respiratory infections. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used to treat various health conditions.
